news.Title: Asia shares slip, oil prices pile pressure on bonds
news.Content:
By Wayne Cole

SYDNEY, May 18 (Reuters) - Asia share markets slipped on Monday as fresh drone attacks in the Gulf pushed up oil prices and bond yields, while the AI boom is set to be tested by earnings from tech-diva Nvidia this week.

A drone strike caused a fire at a nuclear power ‌plant in the United Arab Emirates, while Saudi Arabia reported intercepting three drones, as U.S. President Donald Trump warned that Iran must act "fast" to reach a deal.

More from Yahoo Scout

What caused the surge in oil prices on Monday?

Why are bond yields rising amid geopolitical tensions?

What makes Nvidia earnings crucial for AI market sentiment?

How could the Strait of Hormuz closure affect global inflation?

Meanwhile, ‌the vital Strait of Hormuz remains closed to all but a trickle of shipping as Tehran tries to formalise its control of the waterway that used to carry 20% of the world's oil trade.

"The closure is draining ​global oil inventories fast," warned analysts at Capital Economics. "Inventories could reach critical levels by end-June, setting the stage for Brent at $130-140pb, if not higher."

"If the strait is closed through year-end and oil stays around $150pb into 2027, that would push inflation to near 10% in the UK and euro zone, send rates back to their recent peaks and lead to global recession."

Brent was trading up 1.2% at $110.63 a barrel, while U.S. crude climbed 1.0% to $106.42 a barrel. [O/R]

G7 finance ministers gather in Paris on Monday to discuss the Strait of Hormuz and critical raw material supplies, even as geopolitical differences ‌threaten to test the group's cohesion.

Concerns energy costs would stay high ⁠and thus continue to drive inflation, saw global bond markets hammered on Friday.

Yields on U.S. 10-year notes were up at 4.584%, having surged 23 basis points last week, while 30-year bonds stood at 5.109% after jumping 18 basis points on the week.

Investors in turn feared central banks ⁠globally would have to tighten to head off an inflationary spiral, and a hike from the Federal Reserve is now seen as a 50-50 chance this year.

Minutes of the Fed's last meeting are out on Wednesday and should show how much pressure there was on the committee for a shift to a neutral stance, and away from an easing bias.

Japan's Nikkei eased 0.4%, having fallen 2% last week ​though ​that was from record highs. South Korean stocks fell 2.1%, as the red-hot market cooled just a ​little after demand for semiconductors drove it to all-time peaks.

MSCI's broadest index ‌of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan lost 0.6%. China's markets hit their highest in more than four years last week, but will have to weather data on April retail sales and industrial output later in the session.

AI, RETAIL EARNINGS TO TEST THE BULL RUN

Story Continues

S&P 500 futures fell 0.4% and Nasdaq futures lost 0.5% in early trade.

While Wall Street has been supported by upbeat earnings, analysts at Citi noted half of the boost to earnings came from one-time items such as tariff add-backs and asset mark-ups. Both the gains in profits and the overall indexes were also tightly based.

"We identify 20 stocks that contributed the majority of index earnings upside," wrote analyst Scott Chronert in a note. "Forward guidance increases also show a similar narrow focus."

"Broadening is a ‌necessary condition for meaningful index upside from here," he added. "This will require a better line of sight ​to the Iran conflict wind-down."

The all-important AI trade will be tested by earnings from Nvidia due on Wednesday, where ​expectations are sky high for the world's most valuable company.

Nvidia shares are up ​36% since the March low, while the Philadelphia SE semiconductor index has surged more than 60%, amid voracious demand for chips as tech companies ‌spend massively to build AI-related infrastructure.

Also due this week are results from ​a host of retailers led by Walmart, which ​will provide an insight into how consumers are faring with high energy prices.

In forex markets, risk aversion has tended to benefit the greenback as the world's most liquid currency. The U.S. is also a net energy exporter, giving it a relative advantage over Europe and much of Asia.

The euro sat at $1.1620, after losing 1.4% last week. ​The pound wallowed at $1.3318, having dived 2.3% last week as ‌political instability added to already intense pressure on the gilt market.

The dollar held firm on the yen at 158.64, with only the threat of Japanese intervention ​preventing another speculative assault on the 160.00 chart barrier.

In commodity markets, gold was flat at $4,540 an ounce, having drawn little support so far as a safe ​haven or as a hedge against inflation risks. [GOL/]

(Reporting by Wayne Cole; Editing by Jacqueline Wong)

news.Title: 美科技股回檔台指期夜盤跌　法人：台股波動加大
news.Content:
（中央社記者鍾榮峰台北18日電）美伊衝突未解，國際油價上揚，美股4大指數15日下挫，科技股回檔，台積電ADR跌3.2%，台指期期貨夜盤下跌487點收40511點，台積電期貨夜盤下跌20點收2235點。台股15日盤中再創42408.66點歷史新高後、上下劇烈震盪近1392點，終場失守5日和10日短期均線。

展望本週台股走勢，法人分析，盤勢進入高檔整理期，獲利了結賣壓以及拉回接盤力道拉扯，預期短線震盪波動加大，權值股龍頭台積電仍是觀察指標，外資在期貨空單仍超過5萬口，觀察外資在現貨市場買賣超動向，預期台股在4萬點附近有強勁支撐力道。

展望國際股市，法人指出，人工智慧AI晶片大廠輝達（NVIDIA）將於台灣時間21日公布財報，市場高度關注輝達對AI產業發展最新看法，本週美國聯邦公開市場委員會（FOMC）將公布會議紀要，牽動利率政策走向，此外通膨變數對美債殖利率及美股影響，須密切觀察。

美股道瓊工業指數15日終場下跌537.29點或1.07%，收49526.17點，標準普爾500指數下跌92.74點或1.24%，收7408.50點。

那斯達克指數下跌410.08點或1.54%，收26225.14點，費城半導體指數下跌485.314點或4.02%，收11588.465點。

輝達下挫4.42%收225.32美元，台積電美國存託憑證（ADR）跌3.2%收404.35美元，美光（Micron）收跌6.6%、超微（AMD）跌5.7%、英特爾（Intel）跌近6.2%。

台股15日收41172.36點，下跌579.39點，跌幅1.39%，台股15日收盤較前一週下跌431.58點，跌幅約1.04%。根據台灣證券交易所統計，全體上市公司最新總市值為新台幣134兆2983.73億元，較前一週減少1兆3988.16億元。

在台指期淨部位，三大法人15日淨空單增加1161口至8491口，其中外資淨空單減少105口至5萬963口。（編輯：楊凱翔）1050518

news.Title: How Much Will SpaceX Stock Be Worth by 2030? Here's What History Says.
news.Content:
With whispers of a valuation approaching $2 trillion, SpaceX stands on the precipice of potentially the largest initial public offering in history. Yet forecasting the company's stock price at 2030 feels more like an astrology project than astute financial analysis. SpaceX must overcome regulatory hurdles and make significant technological leaps over the next few years to meet investors' sky-high expectations.

Let's explore where SpaceX stands today, detail the company's vast opportunities, and examine some sobering lessons from recent high-profile IPOs.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Putting SpaceX's valuation into perspective

Let's take a look at SpaceX's valuation trajectory over the last couple of years:

In late 2024, SpaceX bought back shares from employees for $185 each. This secondary share deal valued the company at $350 billion.

About one year later, a tender offer pushed the company's worth to $800 billion by December 2025.

Earlier this year, SpaceX merged with xAI in a $1.25 trillion transaction.

Most recently, shares of SpaceX hit a valuation of $1.5 trillion on Forge Global's private market trading platform.

The company is reportedly eying an IPO valuation between $1.75 trillion and $2 trillion. For context, the company would be valued higher than Walmart , Samsung , Meta Platforms , and Tesla at the high end of this range.

Given SpaceX's S-1 filing remains confidential, investors have only estimates of the company's revenue and profitability. Some analysts estimate that SpaceX generated between $15 and $16 billion in revenue last year, while others put the company's top line closer to $18 billion. Regardless of the precise sales figure, SpaceX's IPO valuation implies a price-to-sales (P/S) multiple exceeding 100.

Separating SpaceX's actual business from its lofty goals

SpaceX currently operates two proven segments with a third, transformative one taking shape. The company's reusable rockets reduce launch costs by orders of magnitude, helping SpaceX capture lucrative contracts across commercial and government sectors. Meanwhile, Starlink has evolved from a niche connectivity provider into a global broadband network.

The real multiplier for SpaceX is artificial intelligence (AI). The company is increasingly marketing itself as an orbital AI infrastructure provider -- leveraging Starlink's network and Starship's launch capacity to deploy data centers in space.

Story Continues

The pitch looks compelling on the surface: Orbital compute can sidestep Earth's power grid and cooling bottlenecks, offering greater scale for training and inference workloads. Reports claim that SpaceX estimates its total addressable market to be around $28 trillion, with the vast majority tied to enterprise AI.

These technology IPOs might be a good proxy for SpaceX's fate

After a direct listing in 2020, Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) faced loads of skepticism over its lumpy, government-heavy revenue and recurring operating losses. A strategic pivot toward commercial AI applications fueled top-line growth and helped widen profit margins. Since bottoming at around $6 per share in 2022, Palantir stock has gained more than 2,100%.

Snowflake (NYSE: SNOW) followed a completely different arc. Its 2020 IPO popped dramatically on the first day of trading. Ultimately, it surrendered these gains due to a high-interest-rate environment and normalized growth rates. Investors who bought near the peak remain in the red years later.

Most recently, Figma (NYSE: FIG) and Cerebras Systems (NASDAQ: CBRS) delivered outsize first-day surges driven by enthusiasm across high-growth software and next-generation chip architectures. Figma's momentum eventually settled into a more measured trajectory, with shares now hovering well below their level in early trading days. For now, it's too early to tell if Cerebras stock will maintain its premium as the company works to convert backlog from OpenAI and Amazon Web Services into durable revenue against larger chip rivals.

History shows that high-profile IPOs generally come with immediate hype and valuation premiums that are driven by narrative. These frothy valuations can persist if business fundamentals compound rapidly and execution matches the growth story -- as with Palantir.

More often, however, IPO stocks erode in the first year after lockups expire, as actual quarterly performance replaces inspiring rhetoric. SpaceX enters the public markets under immense scrutiny. Its IPO will almost certainly price shares at a premium, reflecting the assumed synergies between AI and the final frontier. But the company's valuation in 2030 remains anyone's guess.

Maintaining a trillion-dollar profile will require Starlink to scale to serve tens of millions of users, Starship to achieve routine launches, and orbital AI to evolve from an interesting concept into a revenue-generating business. Execution delays, geopolitical tensions over orbital routes, dependence on Elon Musk, and the possibility that AI compute economics favor ground-based solutions are all genuine risks for SpaceX.

Buying SpaceX at its IPO price requires accepting extreme valuation and volatility risk. A single missed milestone or change in perception could easily trigger a steep correction that lasts several years. Meanwhile, flawless execution from Musk and his team could mint generational wealth. While the stars are within reach, the journey will undoubtedly test even the most patient investors.

We just issued 'double down' alerts on 3 stocks — find out if Tesla made our list

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $584,450 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $57,222 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $469,293 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of May 11, 2026

Adam Spatacco has positions in Amazon, Meta Platforms, Palantir Technologies, and Tesla. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Amazon, Figma, Meta Platforms, Palantir Technologies, Snowflake, Tesla, and Walmart. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

news.Title: 台股「大怒神」創高又急殺　蔡明彥一語道破：狗跑太遠
news.Content:
台股加權指數本月中旬上演劇烈震盪行情，上週五更上演「大怒神」行情，加權指數早盤一度衝上42,408.66點、再創歷史新高，然而盤中賣壓湧現，最低下探41,016.73點，單日高低震盪近1400點。對於盤勢詭異走勢，群益投顧董事長 蔡明彥 以生動比喻揭示真相。他形容：「這就像老人遛狗一樣，狗太興奮了，跑得太遠，當然要拉回來。」

蔡明彥受訪指出，雖然台股波動幅度驚人，但此屬基本面強勁下的技術性修正，投資人不必過度恐慌。他認為，AI產業基本面依舊堅實。包括鴻海、廣達等大型供應鏈廠商對明年資本支出看法極為樂觀，國際大廠對台灣供應鏈的下單動能並未停止，「在AI競賽中，目前還沒有人要喊停」。然而，加權指數創高後隨即急殺的主因，在於股價漲幅早已超前獲利成長。當台股創高、當沖交易金額甚至突破兆元大關時，代表市場投機氣氛過熱，獲利了結賣壓傾巢而出成為「浮額清洗」的必要過程。

台積電短期領先地位無虞。市場先前傳出客戶可能為分散風險而減少對台積電的依賴，蔡明彥直言先進製程目前仍是「極度吃緊」。即便客戶動了分散訂單的念頭，新的代工廠從建廠、裝機到正式量產，至少需花數月甚至半年以上時間，因此短期內台積電在大盤核心的領先地位依然穩固。

國際市場新興變數需要關注。蔡明彥提及美國AI晶片新創企業Cerebras Systems的IPO案，以及未來SpaceX若掛牌後可能與台積電相當的市值，都可能產生資金排擠效應。當大型標的上市，不排除外資會採取「賣舊換新」的策略調度資金，這是後續市場需留意的潛在風險。

此外，記憶體供應吃緊成為下半年隱憂。蔡明彥警告，若記憶體漲勢過快，反過來會壓抑PC與手機需求。在終端消費市場原本就偏向疲弱的背景下，若記憶體價格持續飆升導致產品售價調漲，恐怕會反噬消費力道，成為下半年供應鏈需緊盯的潛在風險。

news.Title: 高瓴Q1押注AI硬體鏈，增持輝達、台積電、英特爾，減碼中概龍頭
news.Content:
【財訊快報／陳孟朔】外電報導，最新13F持倉報告顯示，高瓴資本(Hillhouse Group)第一季明顯加碼AI硬體與半導體供應鏈，增持輝達(Nvidia，美股代碼NVDA)、英特爾(Intel，美股代碼INTC)、台積電(美股代碼TSM)、康寧(Corning，美股代碼GLW)、高意公司(Coherent，美股代碼COHR)與Lumentum，美股代碼LITE)等標的，同時新建倉邁威爾科技(Marvell Technology，美股代碼MRVL)與叮咚買菜。從持倉市值變化來看，邁威爾科技、Arrivent BioPharma、唯品會、台積電、英特爾與輝達，是第一季持倉市值增加最多的個股，顯示資金配置重心明顯向AI算力、光通訊、晶片與數據中心基礎設施傾斜。市場人士指出，高瓴加碼輝達、台積電與英特爾，反映其對AI晶片主線仍維持高度參與。輝達仍是AI加速器需求的核心受益者，台積電則是先進製程與AI晶片代工不可替代的關鍵平台，英特爾雖基本面仍處轉型期，但在美國半導體製造回流、先進封裝與潛在代工訂單題材下，具備估值修復想像。同步增持康寧、Coherent與Lumentum，則凸顯AI數據中心對光通訊、玻璃基板、雷射元件與高速傳輸零組件需求升溫。

值得注意的是，高瓴第一季減持谷歌A股(Alphabet，美股代碼GOOG)、iShares比特幣ETF(IBIT)、阿里巴巴(美股代碼BABA)與拼多多(美股代碼PDD)，但拼多多與阿里巴巴仍是其重倉持股。交易員表示，這代表高瓴並非完全撤出中國網路平台股，而是在中概股與AI硬體鏈之間進行再平衡；其中阿里巴巴、拼多多持倉市值下降最多，可能同時受到減持與股價表現影響，反映資金對中國消費與平台經濟短線彈性仍採取更審慎態度。

分析師認為，高瓴這份13F最大訊號，是其把第一季增量資金集中放在「AI基礎設施」而非單純消費網路平台。從邁威爾、輝達、台積電到Lumentum與Coherent，配置橫跨AI運算晶片、先進製程、光通訊與高速傳輸，顯示機構投資人仍把AI資本開支視為最具確定性的成長主線。不過，在費半短線漲幅已遠離長期均線、全球債息攀升壓抑高估值資產之際，這類加碼也意味後續股價更依賴財報、訂單與AI資本支出能否持續兌現。

news.Title: 橋水基金Q1大舉新進台積電ADR、增持半導體，清倉賽富時等軟體股
news.Content: 【財訊快報／陳孟朔】外電報導，全球最大避險基金橋水聯合基金(Bridgewater Associates)最新公布的13F持倉報告顯示，第一季對旗下美股持倉進行大幅調整，在科技軟體類股與工業、半導體類 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 橋水基金在第一季大幅買入台積電ADR，金額達3.64億美元，顯示看好其在AI與半導體景氣復甦下的前景。
• 橋水基金亦增持亞馬遜、輝達、美光、博通、邁威爾科技等半導體與科技巨頭的持倉。
• 與此同時，橋水基金全面清倉賽富時、Workday、ServiceNow、GoDaddy等多檔企業軟體股，因估值承壓。
• 橋水基金第一季工業類股權重增幅最大，金融類股權重降幅最大，清倉Global Payments和Webster Financial Corp.等金融股。
• ETF仍為橋水基金核心持倉，SPDR標普500 ETF為第一大重倉，但iShares標普500核心ETF本季持倉大幅削減。
news.Keywords: 基金持倉調整,半導體龍頭,AI前景,科技巨頭,軟體股撤資

news.Title: 美國科技股上週五下跌，台股週一可能面臨賣壓
news.Content: 【財訊快報／魏聖峰】美股上週五受到川習會沒有具體成果，30年期美債殖利率升破5.1%、創下近一年新高，加上國際油價同步走高，西德州原油每桶上漲4.2%、收在105.42美元，布蘭特原油每桶上漲3.35%、收在1 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 受川習會無具體成果、美債殖利率創一年新高及國際油價走高影響，上週五美股科技與半導體股領跌。
• 費城半導體指數下跌4.2%，美光、AMD、Nvidia、博通、台積電ADR、日月光ADR均下跌，預示台股週一可能面臨賣壓。
• 建議短線漲多的股票（如記憶體、ABF相關）暫時觀望，金融股可能扮演撐盤角色。
• 花旗減持韓國股市部位，加上三星電子可能罷工，亦可能衝擊亞洲股市。
• 台股上週五呈現開高走低，成交量爆增，聯發科、台達電、台積電、廣達等權值股領跌，留意是否持續遭逢賣壓。
news.Keywords: 美股下跌,獲利了結,殖利率攀升,半導體壓力,台股賣壓

news.Title: Warren Buffett Successor Greg Abel Just More Than Tripled Berkshire's Stake in This "Magnificent Seven" Stock. Is It a Buy?
news.Content: When Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) filed its first quarterly 13-F under new CEO Greg Abel late last week, one move stood out from the rest: In his first three months running the conglomerate, Abel boosted Berkshire's stake in Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) by 224%, lifting the position to nearly 58 million shares worth about $23 billion. That makes the Google parent one of Berkshire's seven largest equity holdings. Of course, the stake was relatively modest ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Berkshire Hathaway, under new CEO Greg Abel, significantly increased its stake in Alphabet by 224%, bringing the total to nearly 58 million shares worth approximately $23 billion.
• Alphabet reported a 22% year-over-year revenue increase to $109.9 billion in Q1, marking the fastest growth pace in over two years and the 11th consecutive quarter of double-digit growth.
• Google Cloud revenue surged 63% to $20 billion with operating margins nearly doubling to 32.9%, driven by Enterprise AI solutions which saw revenue growth of nearly 800% year-over-year.
• Despite strong performance, significant capital expenditures ($35.7 billion in Q1) and raised full-year guidance for 2026 ($180-$190 billion) present potential pressure on free cash flow.
• Alphabet's stock has appreciated approximately 25% year-to-date, trading at a P/E ratio of around 30, which is considered reasonable given its accelerating growth and expanding margins.

news.Title: Nvidia vs. Cerebras: Better AI Stock to Buy Now
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has been the "go-to" artificial intelligence (AI) stock since the start of this technology revolution. Why? Because the company offers the most powerful chips that fuel the most essential of AI operations, such as training models and guiding them through their work. Other chip designers -- market giants such as Advanced Micro Devices and Intel -- also are benefiting from the AI boom, but Nvidia remains the leader. Now, however, a young player is set to challenge this market behemoth, and investors are ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia is highlighted as the dominant leader in AI chips, providing essential components for model training and operations, with revenue reaching $215 billion in the recent full year.
• Nvidia's gross margin exceeds 70%, and the company maintains its market dominance through continuous innovation and broad industry partnerships, including with Nokia for 6G systems.
• Cerebras Systems, a newer player, has challenged Nvidia with larger AI chips that offer significantly faster inference speeds (up to 15x faster than leading GPU-based solutions).
• Cerebras reported revenue growth from $24 million in 2022 to over $510 million last year and secured a multi-year $20 billion deal with OpenAI.
• The article contrasts Nvidia's established market leadership and profitability with Cerebras's disruptive potential and rapid early-stage growth.
news.Keywords: AI chip market, market leadership, competitive landscape, revenue growth, technological innovation

news.Title: Is Micron the Next Nvidia?
news.Content: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) is having a moment. The maker of memory chips just posted second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year over year. Gross margins have scaled up past 70%, a figure that would have seemed absurd for a memory company just three years ago. The stock trades at a forward P/E below 10, while the semiconductor industry median is around 30. If you squint, it ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology reported a significant Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year-over-year, with gross margins exceeding 70%.
• The comparison to Nvidia is tempting due to Micron's role in supplying high-bandwidth memory (HBM), a critical component for AI accelerators, with its capacity sold out through 2026.
• Unlike Nvidia, which designs AI accelerators and controls the CUDA software ecosystem, Micron is a component supplier whose pricing power is subject to supply-demand dynamics and competition from SK Hynix and Samsung.
• Micron's stock trades at a forward P/E below 10, significantly lower than the semiconductor industry median of around 30, suggesting the market applies a risk discount due to the cyclical nature of the memory market.
• While not the 'next Nvidia,' Micron is presented as a strong AI ecosystem supplier with a pristine balance sheet (debt-to-equity of 0.14, $14.6 billion cash), despite potential long-term pricing power limitations.
news.Keywords: HBM supply, AI components, valuation discount, cyclicality, competitive moat

news.Title: What Is the Cheapest "Magnificent Seven" Stock?
news.Content: The "Magnificent Seven" group of stocks includes some of the largest companies in the world, all of which are major players in the tech realm. This group is made up of: Nvidia Alphabet Apple Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) Amazon Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) Tesla All seven of these stocks are trillion-dollar companies by market cap. However, each stock varies in how good a deal ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article analyzes the valuation of 'Magnificent Seven' stocks, focusing on operating cash flow (CFO) as the most relevant metric due to significant capital expenditures.
• Tesla is identified as the most expensive stock, trading at approximately 95 times CFO, while Nvidia is valued at 56 times CFO, still considered expensive but with strong growth potential.
• Meta Platforms stands out as the cheapest, trading at a low 13 times CFO, potentially offering a buying opportunity if its valuation returns to its historical average.
• Microsoft is also highlighted as a potential value play, trading at less than 18 times CFO, below its historical valuation range, despite strong business performance.
• The analysis suggests that while some stocks appear expensive, the overall valuation range for these tech giants based on CFO is within historical norms, indicating a bullish outlook on the sector.
news.Keywords: Magnificent Seven, valuation, operating cash flow, P/E ratio, market sentiment

news.Title: Nvidia Stock Is Poised to Rocket From the Booming Space Economy
news.Content: Nvidia dominates artificial intelligence (AI) chips and infrastructure on Earth – and it's poised to do the same for space applications, both from in-space and Earth, in my view. As such, if you're looking for a " space stock ," you might consider Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) , as the company is growing like gangbusters, extremely profitable, and lower risk than the pure-play space companies. Most of them are burning cash and will need to spend huge sums on capital expenditures for many years. Granted, Nvidia isn't a pure-play space stock, but I consider the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia is positioned to dominate the burgeoning space economy's AI applications, mirroring its success in terrestrial AI markets.
• The global space economy is projected to reach $1.8 trillion by 2035, with the AI in space exploration market expected to grow at a CAGR of 33.4% to $110.2 billion by 2035.
• Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sees significant potential for AI in space, despite current economic challenges for space-based AI data centers.
• Nvidia has launched space AI computing platforms, including the Space-1 Vera Rubin Module, offering up to 25x more AI compute for space-based inferencing compared to previous architectures.
• Several space companies, including Planet Labs, are already utilizing Nvidia's accelerated computing platforms for their next-generation space missions, indicating a first-mover advantage.
news.Keywords: space economy, AI in space, first-mover advantage, satellite technology, future growth

The firm acquired 1,638,700 shares valued at approximately $180 million, according to Tiger Global's ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Intel's stock has surged 404% over the past year, and Tiger Global believes the market is underpricing its potential in the AI era.

news.Title: The AI Stock With a 10-Year Head Start That Wall Street Still Hasn't Fully Priced In
news.Content: As the expenses of building and running artificial intelligence (AI) data centers continue to surge, having lower costs is a huge advantage. One of the best ways to achieve this is with custom chips called application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). ASICs for AI are hardwired chips developed for specific tasks. Not ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOGL) holds a significant advantage in AI due to its custom-designed Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), developed over a decade ago, offering cost and energy efficiency over general-purpose GPUs.
• The company's eighth-generation TPUs, introduced in April, include variations for training (TPU 8t) and inference/agentic AI (TPU 8i), optimizing performance for specific tasks.
• Alphabet's internal infrastructure runs predominantly on TPUs, allowing for lower-cost AI model training (like Gemini) and inference, which benefits its cloud computing segment and other services.
• The company is beginning to offer its TPUs directly to select customers through its partner Broadcom, opening a new potential growth avenue.
• Despite a forward P/E of 28 for 2026 estimates, the market is seen as not fully pricing in Alphabet's complete AI ecosystem (chips, models, infrastructure), making it a buy.
news.Keywords: ASIC advantage, TPU development, cost efficiency, AI infrastructure, cloud computing

news.Title: Snapchat: Don't Rush to Buy This Social Media Stock
news.Content: It's been a tough ride for long-term Snap (NYSE: SNAP) investors. Once touted as a close rival to Meta Platforms ' Instagram, the relatively small social media company remains unprofitable and is down by more than 30% year to date. Investors who are betting on a turnaround ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Snap (SNAP) has experienced a difficult period, with its stock down over 30% year-to-date, and remains unprofitable.
• Snap's revenue growth rate of 8.8% annually over the past three years significantly lags behind Meta Platforms' 19.9% CAGR.
• The company generated less than $10 billion in revenue over the past year, compared to Meta Platforms' over $200 billion, and is less profitable than Pinterest.
• Snap reported a $89 million net loss in Q1 and faces challenges in increasing average revenue per user (ARPU) despite a 5% year-over-year increase in monthly active users.
• Strong competition from Instagram and TikTok, coupled with slow revenue growth, makes Snap a less attractive investment compared to faster-growing, profitable social media giants.
news.Keywords: user growth, ARPU, profitability, competitive pressure, revenue stagnation

news.Title: Where Could Palantir Be in 10 Years? The Bull and Bear Cases.
news.Content: Few large-cap stocks polarize investors the way Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) does right now. The company has gone from being labeled a software consultancy to being considered a provider of essential artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure for governments and large enterprises. A 10-year horizon is the right lens for a stock like this, because the disagreement is not really about next quarter's revenue. It is about whether Palantir becomes the operating system for AI within large organizations or ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is evolving from a software consultancy to a provider of AI infrastructure for governments and large enterprises.
• In Q1 2026, Palantir reported U.S. government revenue of $687 million (up 84% YoY) and raised full-year revenue guidance to 71% growth, with adjusted operating income at $984 million (60% margin).
• The bull case centers on its ontology layer becoming an enterprise AI standard, strong government contract momentum (over $900 million in new federal contracts), and compounding commercial business growth.
• The bear case highlights risks from government contract renewals, potential demand pull-forward, slower international commercial growth, and intense competition from larger players.
• Despite a high revenue multiple, the article suggests Palantir's long-term potential as an AI operating system warrants consideration, though valuation remains a concern.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, government contracts, commercial growth, ontology layer, valuation risk

news.Title: Why Would Anyone Buy SPYM Instead of QQQ?
news.Content: If you've been following the stock market for the past few years, you might assume that tech stocks are always the best place to put your money. Tech stocks always outperform the S&P 500 index, right? The reality is complicated. It's true that the tech-heavy Nasdaq-100 index, which includes America's most well-known tech companies, has outperformed the S&P 500 for the past 15 years. As of June 30, 2025, the Invesco QQQ ETF (NASDAQ: QQQ) , which tracks the Nasdaq-100, had beaten the S&P 500 in seven of the previous 10 years. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our ...
news.AI_Summary:

• QQQ's top holdings include Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, representing about 33% of the fund.
• Investors may consider the SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 ETF (SPYM) as a defensive move due to concerns about tech stock concentration and potential AI hype correction, despite its lower historical returns compared to QQQ.
news.Keywords: ETF, Nasdaq-100, S&P 500, tech stocks, diversification

news.Title: Micron's and Sandisk's Futures Are Heavily Influenced by 2 Foreign Chip Companies. Here's How to Buy Them.

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Where are Amazon’s headquarters? Seattle and beyond
news.Content: Jef Bezos and his wife, MacKenzie Scott, drove from New York City to Seattle to start Amazon.com in a rented garage. They stayed there and made Washington’s biggest city the headquarters for the world’s biggest online retailer. Later on, the company grew, and as part of its expansion, it set up a second headquarters on the East Coast. Here’s where Amazon’s two different headquarters are located and how many people work at each one. Where is Amazon’s headquarters? The area of Amazon’s first and original headquarters, which it ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon's original headquarters are located in the Puget Sound region, encompassing Seattle and neighboring cities like Bellevue and Redmond.
• The company established a second headquarters, known as Metropolitan Park or HQ2, in Arlington, Virginia, to accommodate its growing workforce.
• The Seattle headquarters house over 45,000 workers, while the Arlington location can accommodate up to 14,000 employees.
news.Keywords: headquarters, expansion, workforce, Seattle, Arlington

news.Content: 台股上周衝上歷史新高後，短線過熱壓力快速浮現，上週五盤中劇烈震盪逾1400點，終場下跌579點，收在41172點；同日美股也遭科技股賣壓拖累全面收黑，費城半導體指數大跌4.02%，台積電ADR下跌3.2%，台指期夜盤更下挫4 ...

• 市場回檔主因包含美股科技股賣壓、川習會未達預期、以及近期漲多AI與半導體股的獲利了結。
• 儘管面臨技術面與籌碼面風險，法人認為AI多頭基本面並未改變，營收與獲利表現顯示真實需求存在。
• 外資上週賣超ETF、金融及傳產股，而投信則積極加碼聯電、華邦電、台積電等科技股，顯示資金動向分歧。
• 輝達將於週一公布財報，其表現與展望將牽動AI供應鏈評價及台股科技股後續走勢，COMPUTEX展前資金可能提前布局相關題材。

news.Title: 3 Dividend Stocks That Are Quietly Crushing the Market
news.Content: Which is most associated with income investing: explosive returns or stability? I suspect most people would go with the latter answer. However, it's possible to enjoy both explosive returns and stable dividends. We're seeing that to be the case so far in 2026. Three dividend stocks are quietly crushing the market. And each of them could have more room to run. Will AI ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom (AVGO) has outperformed the market in 2026, showing strong revenue growth in its AI business and a significant increase in dividend payouts over the past decade.
• The company's substantial share repurchase program ($7.8 billion in Q1 2026) complements its dividend growth, supported by strong free cash flow.
• Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) is benefiting from increased global demand for oil and gas and its role in powering AI data centers through natural gas transportation.
• Texas Instruments (TXN) is a top performer year-to-date, focusing on semiconductors for industrial, automotive, and data center markets, and maintains a consistent dividend increase streak.
news.Keywords: Broadcom, AVGO, dividend, AI, Enterprise Products Partners, Texas Instruments, TXN, semiconductor

news.Title: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Wants to Blow Up the Playbook That's Kept Stocks Rising for 15 Years. Here's What Investors Should Do Now.
news.Content: A 15-year boom. That's pretty much what we have had since the Federal Reserve began its second round of quantitative easing (buying U.S. Treasuries) in 2010, following the initial quantitative easing during the Great Recession. This initiative, known as QE2, paved the way for the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) to skyrocket more than 6x. Sure, there were a few bumps along the way. Stocks fell briefly at the outset of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020 and, largely as a result of its aftereffects, again in 2022. However, the Fed's balance sheet served as the quiet engine powering a strong bull ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Federal Reserve's new chair, Kevin Warsh, plans to aggressively reduce the Fed's balance sheet, which currently stands at $6.7 trillion.
• This policy shift could lead to higher bond yields, increased borrowing costs for companies, and potentially lower stock valuations, especially for tech stocks with high multiples.
• Investors are advised to reduce exposure to stocks dependent on quantitative easing, consider increasing exposure to financials, and shift to shorter-duration bonds.
• The change in Fed policy is expected to increase market volatility and uncertainty as investors adjust to a reduced market stabilization role by the central bank.
news.Keywords: Monetary Policy, Federal Reserve, Balance Sheet Reduction, Interest Rates, Market Volatility

news.Title: 夜盤狂瀉487點，台股下周4萬點保衛戰？專家點名3大避風港：這類股先別碰

news.Title: Is It Too Late to Buy Broadcom Stock?
news.Content: Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) has taken investors on a wild ride in 2026. After the stock fell nearly 15% to start 2026, it's now up nearly 30% year to date. However, if you move the start date of the analysis back to 2025, it's up around 90%. That's a huge ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom's stock has seen significant gains, up nearly 30% year-to-date in 2026 and around 90% since 2025.
• The company's valuation appears expensive, trading at 86x trailing earnings and 39x forward earnings.
• A key growth driver is Broadcom's custom AI chips, with CEO Hock Tan projecting over $100 billion in revenue from this segment by 2027.
• The AI semiconductor division generated $8.4 billion in Q1 FY2026, and analysts expect total revenue to reach $159 billion by 2027, justifying the current valuation.
• The stock's future upside depends on the success of its custom AI chip business, with significant growth already priced in.
news.Keywords: AI chips, custom silicon, revenue growth, valuation, semiconductor industry

news.Title: AI boom fuels record memory-chip profits, but cyclical risks loom
news.Content: Investing.com -- Major memory-chip manufacturers are experiencing an unprecedented surge in profitability driven by runaway artificial intelligence demand, according to a Wall Street Journal report published on Saturday. Micron Technology Inc (NASDAQ:MU), which recorded its largest-ever corporate loss just three years ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The memory-chip industry is experiencing unprecedented profitability due to high AI demand.
• Micron Technology (MU) is forecast to become the sixth-most profitable stock in the US, with projected earnings near $100 billion in the next 12 months.
• This turnaround is seen across the sector, benefiting rivals like Samsung and SK Hynix.
• Despite current high profits, the industry is structurally prone to heavy capital expenditure cycles and potential oversupply, leading to cyclical risks.
• Micron is investing heavily in manufacturing expansion, committing $150 billion to new facilities.
news.Keywords: memory chips, AI demand, profitability surge, cyclical risks, capital expenditure

news.Title: 川習會後變天！台股周線翻黑、慘跌431點「牛市死了嗎？」小摩二度調價驚吐真相

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Prediction: This Quantum Computing Stock Will Be Up More Than 20% by the End of 2026
news.Content: Last month, Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) unveiled a family of open-source artificial intelligence (AI) models designed to address calibration and error-correction challenges in quantum computing. Known as Ising , Nvidia's new toolkit helps make fragile qubits more usable by pairing them with the power of graphics processing units (GPUs). The question smart investors ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia has launched 'Ising', an open-source AI model toolkit to address calibration and error-correction in quantum computing.
• This toolkit pairs fragile qubits with GPUs, improving usability and speeding up processor tuning and error correction.
• Nvidia's strategy integrates Ising with its hardware (GPUs) and software (CUDA-Q), creating a tailwind for its existing infrastructure.
• Quantum computing is viewed as a future growth vector, not a near-term catalyst, for Nvidia.
• The analyst predicts Nvidia shares could reach $280 by year-end 2026, representing ~20% upside, driven by AI infrastructure growth (70-80% YoY) and quantum as a call option.
news.Keywords: quantum computing, AI infrastructure, GPU, CUDA-Q, growth vector

news.Keywords: AI, 財報, EPS, 成長股, 產業分化

news.Title: Analyst Predicts Nvidia Stock Should Be 42% Higher
news.Content: Bank of America 's (NYSE: BAC) top semiconductor analyst, Vivek Arya, recently raised Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) price target from $300 to $320, implying roughly 42% upside from its closing price as of May 15. The bullish call is supported by Bank of America's increasingly optimistic view of the AI opportunity. The company has raised its estimate ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bank of America raised Nvidia's price target to $320, implying ~42% upside, driven by an optimistic AI market view.
• The total addressable market for AI data center systems is estimated to reach $1.7 trillion by 2030, with Nvidia expected to capture over 70% of this market.
• Nvidia generated $215.9 billion in FY2026 revenue (up 65% YoY) with a gross margin above 71%.
• Key customers like Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta are expected to spend over $700 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026.

news.Keywords: price target, AI market, revenue growth, gross margin, customer spending

news.Title: Up 231%, Is RTX Proving Why It Was a Mistake for Honeywell to Replace RTX in the Dow Jones Industrial Average?
news.Content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) made headlines on May 14 after closing above 50,000. But the Dow would be even higher if Honeywell International (NASDAQ: HON) hadn't replaced Raytheon Technologies -- now RTX (NYSE: RTX) -- on Aug. 31, 2020. The change was part of a broader shake-up that swapped Salesforce for ExxonMobil and Amgen for Pfizer . news.Title: Intel Is Supposed to Be in a New CPU Era But Its Losing Market Share to AMD and Arm

news.AI_Summary:

• Alphabet reported strong Q1 revenue growth of 22% year-over-year to $109.9 billion, driven by a 63% surge in cloud services to $20 billion and a 203% increase in cloud operating income.

news.Title: Retail Earnings Loom: What Can Investors Expect?
news.Content: Walmart WMT shares have been standout performers this year, handily outperforming not just the broader market indexes but also the likes of Amazon AMZN and many members of the Magnificent 7 group. Walmart and other big-box retailers are undoubtedly facing a difficult operating environment, with elevated fuel costs not only adding to consumers’ financial burdens but also increasing the retailers’ expenses. news.Title: Stocks Settle Sharply Lower as Bond Yields Jump on Inflation Fears
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Friday closed down -1.24%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed down -1.07%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed down -1.54%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) fell -1.26%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) fell -1.56%. Stock indexes sold off sharply on Friday, weighed down by a broad selloff in global bond markets amid soaring crude oil prices that are fueling inflation fears. Doubts over whether oil supplies from the Middle East will normalize anytime soon pushed WTI to a 1.5-week high on Friday, as peace talks between the US ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Major stock indexes (S&P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq 100) closed sharply lower due to a broad selloff in global bond markets.
• Soaring crude oil prices, driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and doubts about supply normalization, fueled inflation fears and pushed bond yields higher.
• Stronger-than-expected US economic data, including the Empire manufacturing survey and manufacturing production, further contributed to rising bond yields.
• The market is discounting a low probability of an FOMC rate cut in the near future due to inflation concerns.
news.Keywords: inflation fears, bond yields, crude oil prices, geopolitical risk, macroeconomic indicators

• Stablecoin transaction volume grew 72% year-over-year to $33 trillion in 2025.

The company now sits at the center of nearly every major AI buildout because the world’s most advanced AI chips cannot be produced at scale without TSMC’s manufacturing and packaging technology. • Record Q3 results and a higher Q4 outlook reflect momentum in leading-edge foundry and HBM-driven DRAM.

news.Title: Is Silicon Motion Technology the Next Sandisk?
news.Content: Memory stocks have boomed during the past year as more investors recognize the connection between AI chips and memory chips. No stock seems to be as hot as Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) , which is up by more than 3,000% during that time. It's also up by almost 500% year to date, so it's natural that some investors are looking for a smaller version of the company. Silicon Motion Technology (NASDAQ: SIMO) may be the answer. It's a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO) produces NAND flash controllers, essential for instructing NAND flash chips used in AI models.
• SIMO reported 23% sequential revenue growth in Q1 and provided a strong forecast for continued high sequential growth throughout the year.
• The company's Q1 revenue of $342.1 million significantly exceeded expectations, leading to shares nearly doubling since the results and tripling year-to-date.
• SIMO's success is tied to the memory solutions boom, driven by AI infrastructure build-out, similar to Sandisk's performance.
• Despite the cyclical nature of the semiconductor industry, AI demand is currently driving significant growth for memory and controller companies.
news.Keywords: NAND flash controllers, AI memory, sequential growth, revenue forecast, semiconductor cycle

news.Title: 聯電推14奈米eHV FinFET 搶OLED驅動IC商機

news.AI_Summary:
• 聯電推出用於顯示驅動 IC 的 14 奈米嵌入式高壓 (eHV) FinFET 技術平台。
• 新製程可提升電源效率、效能，並縮小晶片尺寸，相較 22 奈米製程，功耗降低 40%，晶片面積縮小 35%。
• 此技術有助於提升電池續航力，支援更小型、輕薄的驅動模組設計，適用於高階與摺疊式 OLED 智慧型手機顯示應用。
• 聯電為業界唯一提供最先進 22 奈米顯示驅動 IC 解決方案的晶圓代工廠，此次 14 奈米 eHV 平台是首次將 FinFET 技術導入顯示驅動領域。
news.Keywords: 晶圓代工,OLED驅動IC,FinFET技術,製程節點,功耗降低

news.Title: How AI Nodes Boost Demand for Entegris Consumables
news.Content: Entegris, Inc. ENTG sits in the flow of artificial intelligence-driven node migration, where each new process step raises the penalty for contamination and pushes fabs toward tighter purity control. That dynamic can make consumables demand feel steadier because it tracks wafer starts and process complexity, not just equipment spending. With node transitions increasing “content per wafer,” Entegris is trying to capture more materials intensity in critical steps. The company’s positioning in filtration, selective etch, chemical mechanical planarization, and advanced deposition is central to that playbook. ENTG’s AI-Linked Node Migration Tailwinds As semiconductors move ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Entegris (ENTG) benefits from AI-driven node migration, which increases material intensity and demand for consumables due to tighter purity control.
• The company's positioning in filtration, selective etch, chemical mechanical planarization, and advanced deposition supports higher 'content per wafer'.
• First-quarter 2026 net sales rose 5% year over year to $811.9 million, with unit-driven revenues up approximately 7%.
• Advanced Purity Solutions sales increased 6.8% to $463.6 million, while Materials Solutions revenue rose 2.8% to $351.1 million.
• Revenue exposure is weighted to advanced nodes (40% advanced logic, 30% memory), with a meaningful 2-nanometer production ramp-up expected in 2026.
news.Keywords: AI nodes, consumables, content per wafer, advanced materials, purity control

Headquartered in the Netherlands, the company’s products serve diverse industries, including automotive, wireless infrastructure, lighting, industrial, mobile, consumer, and computing. NXPI has gained 42.1% over the past ...
news.AI_Summary:
• NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) has gained 42.1% over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500.
• In Q1 2026, revenue increased 12% year over year to $3.18 billion, and non-GAAP EPS was $3.05, both exceeding guidance and analyst expectations.
• The company saw improving momentum across all four major end markets, with automotive and Industrial & IoT showing strong growth.
• Analysts expect NXPI's EPS to rise 31.2% year over year to $13.38 for the full fiscal year 2026.
• The consensus rating is a 'Moderate Buy' based on 29 analysts, with 19 'Strong Buy' ratings, though this is more bearish than a month ago.
news.Keywords: semiconductor solutions, automotive, industrial IoT, earnings growth, analyst rating

news.Title: Stocks Settle Mixed on Tech Weakness and Inflation Pressures
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Tuesday closed down -0.16%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed up +0.11%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed down -0.87%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) fell -0.16%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) fell -0.90%. Stock indexes settled mixed on Tuesday. The broader market was under pressure amid weakness in technology stocks, following Monday’s rally that pushed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 to new record highs. The ongoing stalemate in the Middle East between the US and Iran is keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, weighing on market ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The S&P 500 closed down 0.16%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.11%, and the Nasdaq 100 down 0.87% on Tuesday.
• The broader market was pressured by weakness in technology stocks and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.
• US April CPI rose 3.8% year-over-year, stronger than expected and indicating accelerating inflation.
• Hawkish comments from Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee suggested the Fed needs to address services inflation.
• Crude oil prices surged over 4% due to concerns over the Middle East conflict, boosting inflation expectations.
news.Keywords: market sentiment, inflation, geopolitical risk, interest rates, crude oil prices

news.AI_Summary:
